verses.text: Abijah of Judah goes to war against Jeroboam, four hundred thousand against eight hundred thousand. The odds are heavily against him. Yet numbers are not the measure of a battle when the LORD is reckoned on one side.

Abijah of Judah goes to war against Jeroboam, four hundred thousand against eight hundred thousand. The odds are heavily against him. Yet numbers are not the measure of a battle when the LORD is reckoned on one side.

Abijah of Judah goes to war against Jeroboam, four hundred thousand against eight hundred thousand. The odds are heavily against him. Yet numbers are not the measure of a battle when the LORD is reckoned on one side.

Abijah cries from the mount that God gave the kingdom to David and his sons by 'a covenant of salt,' which Jeroboam, helped by worthless men, has seized. He pleads God's enduring promise. What the LORD has covenanted, no rebellion can rightly overturn.

Abijah cries from the mount that God gave the kingdom to David and his sons by 'a covenant of salt,' which Jeroboam, helped by worthless men, has seized. He pleads God's enduring promise. What the LORD has covenanted, no rebellion can rightly overturn.

Abijah cries from the mount that God gave the kingdom to David and his sons by 'a covenant of salt,' which Jeroboam, helped by worthless men, has seized. He pleads God's enduring promise. What the LORD has covenanted, no rebellion can rightly overturn.

Abijah cries from the mount that God gave the kingdom to David and his sons by 'a covenant of salt,' which Jeroboam, helped by worthless men, has seized. He pleads God's enduring promise. What the LORD has covenanted, no rebellion can rightly overturn.

He charges that Israel trusts in golden calves and a multitude, having cast out the LORD's priests and made their own as the heathen do. False worship is their real weakness. A great army cannot cover the ruin of having forsaken the true God.

He charges that Israel trusts in golden calves and a multitude, having cast out the LORD's priests and made their own as the heathen do. False worship is their real weakness. A great army cannot cover the ruin of having forsaken the true God.

'But as for us,' Abijah declares, the LORD is their God, His priests minister, and 'God himself is with us for our captain' — fight not against the LORD. He rests the cause on God's presence. To war against God's people is to war against God Himself.

'But as for us,' Abijah declares, the LORD is their God, His priests minister, and 'God himself is with us for our captain' — fight not against the LORD. He rests the cause on God's presence. To war against God's people is to war against God Himself.

'But as for us,' Abijah declares, the LORD is their God, His priests minister, and 'God himself is with us for our captain' — fight not against the LORD. He rests the cause on God's presence. To war against God's people is to war against God Himself.

Jeroboam sets an ambush, and Judah, surrounded, cries to the LORD while the priests sound the trumpets; God smites Israel before Abijah. The cry of faith turns the trap into a triumph. When His people call, God fights for them.

Jeroboam sets an ambush, and Judah, surrounded, cries to the LORD while the priests sound the trumpets; God smites Israel before Abijah. The cry of faith turns the trap into a triumph. When His people call, God fights for them.

Jeroboam sets an ambush, and Judah, surrounded, cries to the LORD while the priests sound the trumpets; God smites Israel before Abijah. The cry of faith turns the trap into a triumph. When His people call, God fights for them.

Jeroboam sets an ambush, and Judah, surrounded, cries to the LORD while the priests sound the trumpets; God smites Israel before Abijah. The cry of faith turns the trap into a triumph. When His people call, God fights for them.

Five hundred thousand of Israel fall, and Judah prevails because they relied on the LORD God of their fathers; Abijah takes several cities. Reliance, not strength, won the day. The arm that leans on God is never the weaker for it.

Five hundred thousand of Israel fall, and Judah prevails because they relied on the LORD God of their fathers; Abijah takes several cities. Reliance, not strength, won the day. The arm that leans on God is never the weaker for it.

Five hundred thousand of Israel fall, and Judah prevails because they relied on the LORD God of their fathers; Abijah takes several cities. Reliance, not strength, won the day. The arm that leans on God is never the weaker for it.

Jeroboam never recovers his power, for the LORD strikes him, while Abijah grows mighty. God settles the account in His own time. The rebel against heaven wastes away, but the one who trusted is strengthened.
